findings.description: E2E REGRESSION: toast.spec.ts will break. Tests expect Sonner toast ([data-sonner-toast], [role="status"]) on failed login, but code now uses inline setLoginError() with [role="alert"] instead of toast.error(). Both tests ('failed login shows error toast' and 'toast auto-dismisses after timeout') will fail because no Sonner toast is rendered on login failure.
findings.fix: Update toast.spec.ts: change toast selectors to match [role="alert"] for login error, OR keep toast.error() alongside setLoginError() for failed login. Preferred: update the E2E test to match the new inline error pattern.

findings.description: Font preload links will 404. <link rel="preload" href="/fonts/GeistVF.woff2"> points to /fonts/ in public dir, but fonts live in apps/web/src/app/fonts/ and Next.js localFont serves them via hashed URLs (/_next/static/media/...). The preload hrefs don't match actual served paths.
findings.fix: Remove the manual <link preload> tags. The `preload: true` and `display: 'swap'` on localFont() already handle preloading correctly via Next.js built-in font optimization. The manual links are redundant AND broken.

findings.description: Math.random() in render causes hydration mismatch. The decorative dots grid uses `style={{ opacity: Math.random() * 0.7 + 0.3 }}` which generates different values on server vs client, causing React hydration warnings in dev and potential flicker.
findings.fix: Use deterministic pseudo-random based on index: `style={{ opacity: ((i * 7 + 3) % 10) / 10 * 0.7 + 0.3 }}` or precompute an array of fixed opacity values.

findings.description: Forgot password link uses <a href="#"> which adds # to URL on click despite preventDefault. Prefer <button> styled as link for non-navigation actions.
findings.fix: Change to <button type="button" className="text-xs text-[var(--color-accent)] hover:underline..."> and remove href="#" + e.preventDefault().

findings.description: LoginThemeToggle uses different data-testid ('login-theme-toggle') than ThemeToggle ('theme-toggle'). While not technically wrong, the existing theme.spec.ts tests target 'theme-toggle' on the dashboard. No E2E coverage exists for the login page theme toggle.
findings.fix: Either add E2E test for login theme toggle, or document that coverage is deferred.

findings.description: VoyagerLogo uses hardcoded gradient colors (#7c8cf8, #6366f1, #818cf8) and flame colors (#f59e0b, #fbbf24) instead of CSS variables. In a high-contrast or custom theme these won't adapt.
findings.fix: Consider using var(--color-accent) or similar CSS variables for the gradient. Low priority since logo is decorative.

findings.description: login-gradient CSS class uses hardcoded hex colors instead of CSS variables, but since these are background gradients specific to the login page, this is acceptable as a one-off design surface.
The inline error SVG icon could be extracted to a shared ErrorIcon component for reuse. Minor since it's only used in one place currently.

positive: Clean split-screen layout with responsive mobile/desktop handling
Proper use of role="alert" for inline error accessibility
Good hydration-safe pattern in LoginThemeToggle (mounted state)
VoyagerLogo correctly uses aria-hidden="true" for decorative SVG
Loader2 spinner with aria-hidden for loading state
E2E selectors preserved: getByLabel(/email/i), getByLabel(/password/i), getByRole('button', {name: /sign in/i}) all still match
No new dependencies added (lucide-react, next-themes already in package.json)
Proper dark/light mode via existing CSS variable system
Good UX improvement: inline errors are better than toasts for form validation feedback
summary: Good UX improvements overall but 1 HIGH (E2E toast.spec.ts regression) and 2 MEDIUM (broken font preloads, Math.random hydration) block approval. The HIGH is the most critical — failed login no longer produces Sonner toasts, so toast.spec.ts will fail.
